headline: Two Blocs, One Strait
comment: SAMPLE / ILLUSTRATIVE content for the v1 DLS restyle. Country geometry comes from the shared geo layer (admin0 PMTiles) at runtime — no inline geometry. `groups` define the alignment colors + legend; `snapshots` assign countries (ISO-3166 alpha-3) to groups, stepping through a hypothetical shift. `bounds` is the [[w,s],[e,n]] camera frame fit on load. Featured/orientation labels carry `lngLat` (geo-anchored on the live map, so they track pan/zoom) plus `x`/`y` (used only by the offline static-SVG harness). The harness/offline build shows a static SVG fallback that mirrors the live choropleth. Nothing here represents a real assessment of any state's foreign policy.

snapshots.label: Baseline
snapshots.caption: Sample baseline alignment — illustrative only, not a real assessment.

snapshots.label: Shift
snapshots.caption: A sample realignment — one state moves to non-aligned, another joins Bloc B.

snapshots.label: Consolidation
snapshots.caption: A sample consolidation — blocs harden and the non-aligned middle narrows.
